name: What do I do first after an on-the-job accident in Boise Idaho? I'm freaking out.
description: Get medical help now, report to employer within 60 days, file workers' comp claim promptly.[2]

mainEntity.name: What do I do first after an on-the-job accident in Boise Idaho? I'm freaking out.

acceptedAnswer.text: Get medical help now, report to employer within 60 days, file workers' comp claim promptly.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Seek medical attention immediately, even if injuries seem minor, then report the injury to your employer within 60 days under Idaho law. This creates a record tying your injuries to the work accident and starts the workers' comp process for benefits like medical care and wage replacement.[2] Key factors: Idaho's workers' comp is no-fault, covering most job injuries; employer must provide forms; delays risk denial. Notify supervisor in writing, follow up with doctor visits, avoid giving statements to insurance without advice; a lawyer can ensure full benefits. Acting fast preserves your rights.
